faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: How do I add items to my shopping list?
faq.a1: On the main page, click the "Shopping List" menu first. Once the shopping list page is open, type the name of the item you want, then tap it to add it to your monthly shopping list.
faq.q2: Can this app be used without internet?
faq.a2: No. WorthIt requires an active internet connection because our app must connect to the database server in real-time to fetch and compare the latest item prices.
faq.q3: How does the Product Scan feature work?
faq.a3: Press the green Scan icon at the center-bottom of the navigation menu. Point your phone camera at the price tag while you're shopping. Our system will detect the price and perform a smart analysis to determine if it is cheap, fair, or too expensive (Worth It or not).
faq.q4: Where does WorthIt get price comparison data?
faq.a4: Our system routinely collects and compares price data from various sources, including major supermarkets to traditional markets, to provide the best savings recommendations for you.
faq.q5: How do I set a monthly budget limit?
faq.a5: Open the "Profile" menu in the top right corner, then select "Budget" in the Shopping Settings section. Enter your spending limit for this month and save.
faq.q6: How to disable budget limit notifications?
faq.a6: Open the "Profile" menu, find the Shopping Settings section, then turn off the toggle (switch) on the "Shopping List Reminder" menu.
faq.q7: Will my shopping data be automatically deleted?
faq.a7: Yes. To maintain app performance, your shopping data and lists are saved monthly and will be automatically reset/deleted at the beginning of each new month.
faq.q8: How many scan results can I save?
faq.a8: WorthIt saves up to 20 of your last product scanning histories. This makes it easy for you to review our smart analysis results on items you've encountered before without needing to scan again.

terms.s1_body: The main purpose of WorthIt is to help users determine whether the price of an item is cheap, fair, or expensive. This is done through our data aggregation and smart analysis system. As a complement, the app also provides supporting features such as shopping list recording, history, and monthly budget management.
terms.s2_body: Price data presented in WorthIt is automatically collected from various trusted sources, such as government databases and supermarket networks, to provide the best price guidance for you. We recognize that market prices can fluctuate. Therefore, our smart system is designed to continuously update data periodically. If you find significant price differences in the field, we are very open and ready to assist you through the 'Contact Us' service. Your input is invaluable for WorthIt to continue presenting increasingly precise data and help you save as much as possible.
terms.s3_body: Users agree to use the WorthIt application wisely. Although our system presents optimal price analysis to help you save, the final decision in every shopping transaction is entirely in your hands. Additionally, you are fully responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account information and device from unauthorized access.
terms.s4_body: The WorthIt team continuously innovates to provide a better user experience. Therefore, we reserve the right to add, modify, or temporarily suspend certain service features in the future. We will always strive to provide reasonable notice if there are major updates that directly impact how you use this application.

privacy.s1_body: We collect your basic information such as Username, Email, and Budget Limit settings. Additionally, to provide optimal price analysis, we also store your Shopping List, last Shopping Activity History, and product Scan History.
privacy.s2_body: To ensure your data is not lost and always synchronized, all information is stored centrally and securely in our server (cloud database). This allows you to continue accessing your shopping data anytime, even when you switch devices.
privacy.s3_body: We implement the latest security standards to protect your data from unauthorized access, modification, or leakage. We are fully committed to protecting your privacy and will never sell or share your personal information to third parties without your explicit permission.
privacy.s4_body: You have full control over your information in WorthIt. You have the right to change your profile, set budget limits, delete certain history, and request permanent deletion of your account and all its data through the app settings menu.

about.title:
About
Us
about.intro: WorthIt is a smart shopping assistant designed to help you get the best price for every item you need. Through historical data aggregation and smart analysis, we compare price fluctuations from various market sources to ensure every shopping decision you make is always right, economical, and planned.
about.vision_mission_body:
Our vision is to create a transparent shopping ecosystem, where every consumer has control over their financial decisions without being trapped by information asymmetry, discount illusions, or shrinkflation. We envision a financially savvy society, starting from the store shelf.

To achieve this, our mission is to deliver an in-store decision engine that works in seconds. Through aggregation of historical price records, market trend predictions, and a smart anomaly detection system, we equip users with instant instructions to buy, find substitutes, or delay purchases right from their hands.
about.why_worthit_body: The phrase 'Worth It' means deserving or worthwhile. We believe every rupiah you spend should deliver maximum value. WorthIt is here for everyone who wants to achieve a healthy financial future, starting with a smart shopping cart.
about.cta_tagline:
Track, Compare,
and Save Together with Us
about.copyright:
© 2026 WorthIt Team. All Rights Reserved.
Made with dedication in Semarang.

faq_q_record_activity: How do I record my shopping activity?
faq_a_record_activity: You can record your shopping activity by tapping the 'I Bought This' button at the bottom of the Analysis Result page after scanning a product, or through your scan history list.
faq_q_export_pdf: How do I export my monthly expenses to a PDF report?
faq_a_export_pdf: Go to the 'Expenses' tab at the bottom navigation menu, then tap the PDF icon at the top right corner of the screen to download your shopping summary for the month.

statistics.insight_stable: Your spending this month is still under control. Keep monitoring it.
statistics.insight_month_down: This month's spending is down {percent}% from last month. Keep it up.
statistics.insight_month_up: This month's spending is up {percent}% from last month. Try to tighten the next purchases.
statistics.insight_category_down: {category} spending is down {percent}% from last month.
statistics.insight_category_up: {category} spending is up {percent}% from last month.
statistics.trend_highest_month: The trend shows your highest spending was in {month}.
statistics.trend_not_enough_data: There is not enough trend data to analyze yet.

explanations.wma_below: The scanned price {scan} is {delta}% below the {period}-month normal price around {normal}, so the value is attractive.
explanations.wma_fair: The scanned price {scan} is close to the {period}-month normal price around {normal}, so it is still reasonable.
explanations.wma_above: The scanned price {scan} is {delta}% above the {period}-month normal price around {normal}, so consider it carefully.
explanations.wma_expensive: The scanned price {scan} is {delta}% above the {period}-month normal price around {normal}, which is a strong expensive signal.
explanations.sr_support: The price is near support at {support}, a lower historical area from the last {period} months.
explanations.sr_resistance: The price is near resistance at {resistance}, a higher historical area from the last {period} months.
explanations.sr_middle: The price sits between support {support} and resistance {resistance} from the last {period} months.
explanations.urgency_1_worthit: The price is good enough to buy for stock even if you do not need it urgently.
explanations.urgency_1_waspada: Because the need is not urgent, delaying is safer unless you specifically want to stock up.
explanations.urgency_1_mahal: The need is low and the price is high, so delaying the purchase is recommended.
explanations.urgency_2_worthit: The need is moderate and the price is friendly, so buying now is reasonable.
explanations.urgency_2_waspada: The price is close to the fair limit. Buy only if your stock is running low.
explanations.urgency_2_mahal: The price is high. If the need can wait, delaying for a few days is better.
explanations.urgency_3_worthit: The need is urgent and the price is still good, so buying now is safe.
explanations.urgency_3_waspada: The need is urgent, and this price is still within an acceptable limit.
explanations.urgency_3_mahal: The price is high, but if the need is urgent, buy only enough for now.
explanations.anomaly_detected: Price anomaly detected: the scanned price exceeds the fair upper bound {fairUpper} from the last {period} months.
explanations.anomaly_clear: No price anomaly detected. The movement is still within a normal historical range.
explanations.shrinkflation_detected: Shrinkflation detected: size is down about {weightDrop}% while unit price is up about {unitRise}%.
explanations.shrinkflation_clear: No shrinkflation detected. The size and unit value are still consistent with historical variants.
explanations.pro_locked: Price anomaly and shrinkflation are advanced Pro checks. Free analysis still includes WMA and historical range.

launch_target_unavailable: The target app is not available on this device.
